pattern: Track the last positions where the minimum bound, the maximum bound, and any out-of-range value appeared; the count of valid subarrays ending at each index follows directly from those three markers.
hints: A subarray is valid only if it contains at least one minK, at least one maxK, and no value outside [minK, maxK].
Sweep left to right remembering the most recent index of a value below minK or above maxK (a wall you cannot cross).
For each right end, also remember the latest index of minK and of maxK; the number of valid starts is max(0, min(lastMin, lastMax) - lastBad).

edge_inputs.description: all equal to a shared bound counts every subarray
out-of-range value splits the array into segments
only one bound ever appears so nothing counts
minK equals maxK but a larger value blocks runs

stress.description: large array with values clustered around the bounds plus occasional out-of-range walls
stress.note: mix guarantees plenty of valid and invalid segments
